video_script.click: button:has-text('Turn their objection into the ad')

video_script.caption: Share buttons pre-fill captions with a ConnectsBlue backlink

screenshot_shots.description: Hero landing with live counter and Wrapped-style preview card
10-question quiz in progress with progress bar
Result screen with Spotify-Wrapped-style card, Claude/fallback blurb, share panel
Live archetype distribution leaderboard with rarest/most-common highlights
All 12 archetypes in the canon grid
Archetype detail page with stats and matchups
Dream-team compatibility builder with two archetype pickers

submission.inspiration: We looked at ConnectsBlue and saw a popularity contest where the voters are the competitors. We also noticed that personality quizzes are the single most battle-tested viral mechanic in internet history — Tickle hit 150M users and Spotify Wrapped had 200M+ people interact in the first 24 hours of 2025. So we inverted the ask. Instead of begging 1,000 fellow marketers for a vote, we hand them a piece of identity content about themselves that they voluntarily broadcast to their professional network.
submission.what_it_does: Marketer DNA is a 60-second, 10-question quiz that types the taker as one of 12 marketer archetypes — The Hook Gremlin, The Dashboard Monk, The LinkedIn Philosopher, and nine more. Each result is a screenshot-perfect 9:16 Spotify-Wrapped-style card with an AI-personalized 3-line roast blurb, a superpower/kryptonite stat bar, and one-click share to X, LinkedIn, WhatsApp, and Threads — every caption pre-filled with a link back to our ConnectsBlue submission.
submission.how_we_built_it: Next.js 14 App Router + Tailwind + TypeScript on a single codebase. Claude Haiku 4.5 generates the personalized blurbs via an /api/roast route that reads the user's key from an x-user-anthropic-key header (never stored server-side) and falls back to hand-written, Forer-effect-grade canonical blurbs when no key is present. @vercel/og renders the 1080×1920 share card PNG at the edge. An in-memory stats store seeded with 1,047 plausible responses powers the live leaderboard without needing a database. A settings page persists API keys in localStorage and links directly to each provider's signup.
submission.challenges: The hardest part wasn't code — it was the copy. Archetype names, taglines, and roast blurbs needed to land in the sweet spot of 90% flattering / 10% playfully self-deprecating so people would actually share them. We hand-edited all 12 archetypes and 10 questions until every forced-choice felt like a specific marketer we knew. The second hard problem was the share loop: every share caption needed to look organic, tag the archetype, and drive a backlink without reading like a call-to-action.
submission.accomplishments: A complete viral loop that works end-to-end with zero external dependencies out-of-the-box. Result cards that render in under a second. A leaderboard that looks populated from first page-load. And copywriting that our early testers screenshot-and-send instead of politely closing.
submission.what_we_learned: Self-disclosure activates the same dopamine circuit as food and money — so if your hackathon asset is about the voter, you don't need to ask for the vote. We also learned that Satori (the engine behind @vercel/og) requires explicit display:flex on every multi-child div, which is the kind of tiny footgun that eats an hour if you're not ready for it.
submission.whats_next: 1) Wire the in-memory stats store to Supabase for cross-device persistence. 2) Add a third AI-generated hook variant for each share caption so the same archetype doesn't post the same tweet twice. 3) A ConnectsBlue referral tracker that attributes votes back to the taker who brought them. 4) A 'sibling archetype' explanation — why you're 72% Hook Gremlin, 18% Meme Whisperer, 10% Viral Savant. 5) An admin export of the raw answer matrix for trend reports.
submission.category_hint: Marketing / viral loop single-page web experience
